campaigns.title: List campaigns for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: If specified, only campaigns with this state will be returned.

schema.title: Campaign state
schema.description: Indicates whether a campaign is open or closed

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results.

campaigns.descriptionHTML:
Lists campaigns in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

example.name: Critical CodeQL alert
example.description: Address critical alerts before they are exploited to prevent breaches, protect sensitive data, and mitigate financial and reputational damage.

example.name: Mitre top 10 KEV
example.description: Remediate the MITRE Top 10 KEV (Known Exploited Vulnerabilities) to enhance security by addressing vulnerabilities actively exploited by attackers. This reduces risk, prevents breaches and can help protect sensitive data.

items.title: Campaign summary
items.description: The campaign metadata and alert stats.

number.description: The number of the newly created campaign

created_at.description: The date and time the campaign was created, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

updated_at.description: The date and time the campaign was last updated, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

items.title: Team
items.description: Groups of organization members that gives permissions on specified repositories.

type.description: The ownership type of the team

organization_id.description: Unique identifier of the organization to which this team belongs

enterprise_id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise to which this team belongs

anyOf.title: Team Simple
anyOf.description: Groups of organization members that gives permissions on specified repositories.

permission.description: Permission that the team will have for its repositories

privacy.description: The level of privacy this team should have

notification_setting.description: The notification setting the team has set

ldap_dn.description: Distinguished Name (DN) that team maps to within LDAP environment

type.description: The ownership type of the team

organization_id.description: Unique identifier of the organization to which this team belongs

enterprise_id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise to which this team belongs

published_at.description: The date and time the campaign was published, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

ends_at.description: The date and time the campaign has ended, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

closed_at.description: The date and time the campaign was closed, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. Will be null if the campaign is still open.

state.title: Campaign state
state.description: Indicates whether a campaign is open or closed

contact_link.description: The contact link of the campaign.

campaigns.title: Create a campaign for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

campaigns.descriptionHTML:
Create a campaign for an organization.

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens must have the "Code scanning alerts" repository permissions (read) on all repositories included in the campaign.

bodyParameters.name: Critical CodeQL alerts
bodyParameters.description: Address critical alerts before they are exploited to prevent breaches, protect sensitive data, and mitigate financial and reputational damage.

example.name: Critical CodeQL alert
example.description: Address critical alerts before they are exploited to prevent breaches, protect sensitive data, and mitigate financial and reputational damage.

campaigns.title: Update a campaign

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: managers
bodyParameters.description: The logins of the users to set as the campaign managers. At this time, only a single manager can be supplied.

bodyParameters.name: team_managers
bodyParameters.description: The slugs of the teams to set as the campaign managers.

bodyParameters.name: ends_at
bodyParameters.description: The end date and time of the campaign, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

bodyParameters.name: contact_link
bodyParameters.description: The contact link of the campaign. Must be a URI.

bodyParameters.name: state
bodyParameters.description: Indicates whether a campaign is open or closed

campaigns.descriptionHTML:
Updates a campaign in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint.

example.description: Address critical alerts before they are exploited to prevent breaches, protect sensitive data, and mitigate financial and reputational damage.
